Surely the Lord God will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret to his servants the prophets.—Amos*

Throughout history when God has found a people that would work with him, it has been through revelation to his prophets. (Apostles are prophets who also serve as witnesses of Christ.) The record of this revelation is scripture. Amos 3:7 reads: "Surely the Lord God will do NOTHING except he revealeth his secret unto his servants the prophets." (Nothing means nothing – not a church, not a ministry,…

At the request of my brethren, I arise to offer a few remarks. And in order to give them to some extent the character of a sermon, I will read the seventh verse of the third chapter of the Prophet Amos—“Surely the Lord will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret unto his servants the prophets.” It is my design merely to draw the attention of our friends to the subject

This verse attests that Israel's predicament is serious, especially as one recalls the promise of the Lord recorded by one of Isaiah's contemporary prophets: "Surely the Lord God will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret unto his servants the prophets." (Amos 3:7.) If there are no prophets remaining in Israel, who will warn the people and call them to repentance? Israel will be left as "blind…
